evals.prompt: I need to migrate our Node.js microservice from kafkajs to the Confluent JavaScript client. The app has a producer that sends order events and a consumer group that processes them. We're running on Confluent Cloud with SASL/PLAIN auth. Here's our main config file that creates the Kafka client with brokers, ssl, and sasl options.
evals.expected_output: Migration plan covering import changes, kafkaJS config wrapping, producer and consumer adjustments, package swap, and verification. Should check Node.js version compatibility first.

assertions.name: Checks Node.js version compatibility
assertions.description: Should run node -v and npm view @confluentinc/kafka-javascript engines to verify the user's Node version is supported before starting migration

assertions.name: Changes import statement
assertions.description: Should change require('kafkajs') to require('@confluentinc/kafka-javascript').KafkaJS

assertions.name: Wraps Kafka client config in kafkaJS block
assertions.description: Should produce new Kafka({ kafkaJS: { brokers: [...], ssl: ..., sasl: {...}, clientId: '...' } }) — NOT new Kafka({ brokers: [...] })

assertions.name: Moves producer acks/compression/timeout to init
assertions.description: If acks, compression, or timeout are set per send(), should move them to kafka.producer({ kafkaJS: { ... } })

assertions.name: Moves consumer fromBeginning to init
assertions.description: If fromBeginning is set in subscribe(), should move it to kafka.consumer({ kafkaJS: { fromBeginning: true } })

assertions.name: Moves autoCommit/autoCommitInterval to consumer init
assertions.description: If autoCommit or autoCommitInterval are set in run(), should move them to kafka.consumer({ kafkaJS: { ... } })

assertions.name: Shows diff and explains each change
assertions.description: Should present proposed changes in diff format (```diff blocks) with a 'Why' section and a 'What to know' section for each change, NOT apply edits silently

assertions.name: Runs package swap
assertions.description: Should run npm uninstall kafkajs && npm install @confluentinc/kafka-javascript and verify package.json updated

evals.prompt: We have a KafkaJS consumer that uses eachBatch with heartbeat() calls, autoCommit in run(), and consumer.on() event listeners for logging rebalance events. It also calls consumer.stop() on shutdown. Can you migrate this to the Confluent JS client?
evals.expected_output: Migration that removes heartbeat() calls, consumer.on() events, consumer.stop(), moves autoCommit to init, and explains each removal with behavior context.

assertions.name: Removes heartbeat() calls from eachBatch callback
assertions.description: Should remove await heartbeat() calls and explain that heartbeats are automatic in confluent-kafka-javascript

assertions.name: Removes consumer.on() event listeners
assertions.description: Should remove consumer.on('consumer.group_join', ...) and similar event listeners and explain they are not supported

assertions.name: Replaces consumer.stop() with consumer.disconnect()
assertions.description: Should replace consumer.stop() with consumer.disconnect() and explain that stop() is not supported

assertions.name: Moves autoCommit from run() to consumer init
assertions.description: Should move autoCommit and autoCommitInterval from run() options to kafka.consumer({ kafkaJS: { autoCommit: ..., autoCommitInterval: ... } })

assertions.name: Wraps groupId in kafkaJS block
assertions.description: Should produce kafka.consumer({ kafkaJS: { groupId: '...' } }) — NOT kafka.consumer({ groupId: '...' })

evals.prompt: Our KafkaJS app uses SSL with custom certificates - we pass ca, key, and cert buffers in the ssl config object. How does this change with the Confluent client?
evals.expected_output: Migration showing ssl becomes boolean-only inside kafkaJS block, certificate paths move outside as librdkafka properties.

assertions.name: Sets ssl to boolean true in kafkaJS block
assertions.description: Should change ssl from an object with ca/key/cert to ssl: true inside the kafkaJS block

assertions.name: Adds certificate paths as librdkafka properties
assertions.description: Should add ssl.ca.location, ssl.certificate.location, and ssl.key.location outside the kafkaJS block

assertions.name: Explains file paths vs buffer content difference
assertions.description: Should explain that the Confluent client uses file paths (ssl.ca.location) instead of reading certificate content into buffers with fs.readFileSync

evals.prompt: I'm migrating a KafkaJS admin client that creates topics with waitForLeaders: true and validateOnly: true. It also uses fetchOffsets with resolveOffsets. What changes are needed?
evals.expected_output: Migration that removes unsupported admin options and explains what is no longer available.

assertions.name: Removes waitForLeaders from createTopics
assertions.description: Should remove waitForLeaders: true and explain it is not supported in confluent-kafka-javascript

assertions.name: Removes validateOnly from createTopics
assertions.description: Should remove validateOnly: true from admin.createTopics() call — NOT supported in @confluentinc/kafka-javascript

assertions.name: Removes resolveOffsets from fetchOffsets
assertions.description: Should remove resolveOffsets and explain it is not yet supported, noting requireStableOffsets as an alternative option

evals.prompt: Our KafkaJS error handling uses instanceof checks like error instanceof KafkaJSNumberOfRetriesExceeded and error.name checks. How should I update this for the Confluent client?
evals.expected_output: Migration that replaces instanceof/name checks with Kafka.isKafkaJSError() and error.code/error.fatal pattern.

assertions.name: Replaces instanceof with Kafka.isKafkaJSError()
assertions.description: Should replace error instanceof KafkaJSError with Kafka.isKafkaJSError(error)

assertions.name: Replaces error.name with error.code
assertions.description: Should replace error.name string checks with error.code comparisons using Kafka.ErrorCode constants

assertions.name: Explains removed error classes
assertions.description: Should list error classes that no longer exist (KafkaJSNumberOfRetriesExceeded, KafkaJSBrokerNotFound, etc.) and explain librdkafka handles them automatically

assertions.name: Introduces error.fatal property
assertions.description: Should explain the new error.fatal boolean and recommend checking it for unrecoverable errors

evals.prompt: We're migrating a high-throughput KafkaJS producer that sends 50k messages per second. Each send is awaited individually. After switching to the Confluent client our throughput dropped dramatically. What's going on?
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis of the producer batching issue with fire-and-forget + flush pattern as the fix, plus linger.ms guidance.

assertions.name: Identifies awaiting each send as the root cause
assertions.description: Should identify that 'await producer.send()' per message defeats librdkafka batching — references GitHub Issue #42 or the 1000x slowdown

assertions.name: Recommends fire-and-forget plus flush pattern
assertions.description: Should show the pattern of calling send() without await in a loop, then await producer.flush({ timeout: ... })

assertions.name: Explains linger.ms option for per-message awaiting
assertions.description: Should mention setting linger.ms to 0 outside the kafkaJS block if the user must await each send

assertions.name: References performance guidance
assertions.description: Should read or reference references/performance-guidance.md for additional tuning recommendations

evals.prompt: My KafkaJS transactional producer uses sendOffsets with consumerGroupId to commit offsets in the transaction. How do I migrate this?
evals.expected_output: Migration showing sendOffsets now takes the consumer object instead of consumerGroupId.

assertions.name: Replaces consumerGroupId with consumer object
assertions.description: Should change sendOffsets({ consumerGroupId: '...' }) to sendOffsets({ consumer }) passing the actual consumer instance

assertions.name: Wraps transactionalId in kafkaJS block
assertions.description: Should show transactionalId moving into kafka.producer({ kafkaJS: { transactionalId: '...' } })

evals.prompt: After migrating to the Confluent JS client, our Kubernetes pods are using 3x more memory than with KafkaJS. The consumers used to sit at 500MB and now they're at 1.5GB. Is this a memory leak?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill. This is a post-migration infrastructure/operations issue, not a code migration.

assertions.name: Does NOT perform a code migration
assertions.description: Should recognize this is a post-migration infrastructure troubleshooting question (Kubernetes memory tuning), NOT a KafkaJS code migration request

evals.prompt: Our Kafka consumers are slow and we're seeing high consumer lag. We're using the Confluent JavaScript client already. How can we tune performance?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill. The user is already on the Confluent client and needs performance tuning, not a migration from KafkaJS.

assertions.name: Does NOT perform a KafkaJS migration
assertions.description: Should recognize the user is already using @confluentinc/kafka-javascript (NOT migrating from KafkaJS) and either decline or redirect to general Confluent client performance tuning documentation

evals.prompt: I want to build a new Kafka producer in Node.js from scratch using the Confluent JavaScript client. I don't have any existing code.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill. This is a greenfield project, not a migration from KafkaJS.

assertions.name: Does NOT perform a migration
assertions.description: Should recognize this is not a KafkaJS migration and either decline to use this skill or redirect the user to general Confluent JS client documentation

evals.prompt: I need to migrate our Node.js app from node-rdkafka to the Confluent JavaScript client. We're using the callback-based API.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger this skill. This is a node-rdkafka migration, not KafkaJS.

assertions.name: Does NOT apply KafkaJS migration patterns
assertions.description: Should recognize this is a node-rdkafka migration (not KafkaJS) and either decline or explain that node-rdkafka migration only requires changing the import statement
